scenes.title: Regular Polygon
scenes.description:
This scene tests the `regular` substructure available on both `animated_polygon` and `polygon`.

**When to use `animated_polygon` with `regular`**: when the number of sides, radius, center, or rotation must respond to a slider or change over time. Every property inside `regular` is a live expression evaluated each frame.

**When to use `polygon` with `regular`**: when the polygon is fixed — its shape is known at scene-load time and never changes. Zero per-frame cost; geometry is built once.

**Rule of thumb**: start with `polygon`; switch to `animated_polygon` only when you need interactivity or animation.

steps.title: animated_polygon — Live N slider
steps.description:
Uses `animated_polygon` with `regular`. All fields inside `regular` are **expression strings** — they can reference sliders, time `t`, or any scene function.

Drag **N** to change the number of sides live. `fill_opacity`, `outline_width`, and `outline_opacity` are also expressions driving the material each frame.

```json
{
  "type": "animated_polygon",
  "regular": { "n": "N", "radius": "1", "center": ["0","0","0"], "rotation": "0" },
  "opacity": "fill_opacity",
  "outlineWidth": "outline_width",
  "outlineOpacity": "outline_opacity"
}
```

steps.title: animated_polygon — All regular parameters
polygon — Static, zero per-frame cost
steps.description:
Uses the static `polygon` element with `regular`. Fields inside `regular` are **plain numbers**, not expressions. Geometry is built once at scene load and never rebuilt — no updater, no expression evaluation, no GPU upload after init.

Use this for fixed reference shapes, background geometry, or any polygon whose shape is determined by the scene design rather than user interaction.

```json
{
  "type": "polygon",
  "regular": { "n": 6, "radius": 0.8, "center": [0, 0, 0], "rotation": 0 },
  "outlineWidth": 2
}
```

info.content: Static polygons — triangle ($N=3$), hexagon ($N=6$), octagon ($N=8$). No sliders, no animation.
